Play a single game between two policies

Source: R/tsr_simulate.R
tsr_play_game.Rd

Simulates one complete 4D tic-tac-toe game with the given policies for player X and player O. Returns a tsr_game object recording the move sequence and outcome.

Usage

tsr_play_game(policy_x, policy_o, seed = NULL)

Arguments

policy_x

Policy for player X. Either a built-in string or a function (board) -> integer cell index.

policy_o

Policy for player O. Same forms as policy_x.

seed

Optional integer. If supplied, RNG is seeded via withr::local_seed(); global .Random.seed is unchanged.

Value

A tsr_game object: list with moves (integer vector of cell indices, in order), winner (0/1/2), n_moves (integer), policies (named character c(x, o)), final_board (a ttt_board), to_move (integer vector — moving player at each ply).

Details

Built-in policy strings: "random", "greedy", "ai" (depth 2), or "aiN" for N in 1:4 (e.g. "ai3").

Examples

g <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 1L)
g$winner
#> [1] 2

On this page
